sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Villarreal comes into this match in strong form, sitting third in the league with 38 points and having won 3 of their last 5 matches, including a 3-1 victory over Elche. Deportivo Alavés, positioned 14th with 19 points, has struggled recently, managing only 2 wins in their last 5 games, with a notable 3-0 loss to Osasuna.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these teams, results are split with Villarreal winning 3-0 at home in November 2024, while Alavés secured a narrow 1-0 victory in March 2025. Historically, matches between these sides often see both teams scoring, with 8 of the last 10 games featuring goals from both.

sections.subtitle: Team Strengths and Weaknesses
sections.text: Villarreal boasts a potent attack, ranking third in goals scored (34) this season, and excels in creating big chances (52, 4th in the league). Alavés struggles offensively with only 15 goals scored, but their defense has shown moments of resilience, especially in tight home games.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Villarreal, Alberto Moleiro (7 goals) and Dani Parejo (standout in head-to-heads with a 7.54 average rating) will be crucial. Alavés relies on Carlos Vicente (4 goals) and goalkeeper Antonio Sivera, who has been in form with a 7.52 rating over the last 5 matches.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Villarreal’s superior league position, attacking prowess, and home advantage at Estadio de la Cerámica, they are heavily favored with Sofascore predicting a 66% chance of a home win. Alavés may struggle to keep up, especially considering their poor away form and Villarreal’s recent dominance at home against them.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Villarreal and Deportivo Alavés ended in a goalless draw, with neither side managing to break the deadlock. Both teams showed moments of promise but lacked the clinical edge needed to convert their chances. Villarreal, playing at home, had a slight edge in terms of territorial dominance with more entries into the final third and penalty area. However, Deportivo Alavés controlled possession slightly better and created more dangerous opportunities, as evidenced by their shots on target and higher expected goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attacking momentum
halftimeSections.text: The game started with an early shift in momentum as Deportivo Alavés took control in the opening minutes with sustained attacking spells. Villarreal responded soon after with a prolonged period of dominance, pushing forward with intent through the middle of the first half. Momentum swung back to Alavés around the midway point, as they mounted significant pressure with several threatening attacks. Towards the end of the half, Villarreal regained control, finishing strongly with renewed energy in their attacking play.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance
halftimeSections.text: Villarreal showed strength in duels, winning more aerial and ground challenges compared to their opponents. Their ability to penetrate the final third frequently suggests they have the tools to unlock Alavés' defense, though their lack of shots on target indicates a need for sharper finishing. On the other hand, Deportivo Alavés demonstrated a more direct approach, with a couple of shots on target and a higher expected goals figure. However, their two yellow cards in the first half hint at potential disciplinary issues that could impact their game plan if not addressed.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players
halftimeSections.text: For Villarreal, Juan Foyth stood out defensively with a high rating, contributing through clearances, tackles, and interceptions. In attack, Georges Mikautadze showed promise as a potential goal threat. For Alavés, Pablo Ibáñez impressed with a strong all-around performance, including a shot on target and solid defensive work. Toni Martínez also posed a constant danger in the final third and could be pivotal if given the right opportunities in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Villarreal push harder for the opener, leveraging their home advantage and ability to dominate duels. They will need to convert their final third entries into tangible chances. Deportivo Alavés, however, will look to capitalize on their direct approach and exploit any defensive lapses from Villarreal. Given their higher expected goals in the first half, they might fancy their chances of striking first if they maintain composure. The game remains finely poised, and tactical adjustments at half-time could play a significant role in deciding the outcome.
